questions.question: A number is divisible by both 12 and 18. What is the smallest positive integer that must divide this number?
questions.explanation: Find LCM(12, 18). Factor: 12 = 2² × 3, 18 = 2 × 3². LCM = 2² × 3² = 36. Any number divisible by both 12 and 18 must be a multiple of 36.

questions.question: Two gears have 48 and 36 teeth. If they mesh and rotate together, after how many rotations of the smaller gear will both gears return to their starting position simultaneously for the first time?
questions.explanation: Find LCM(48, 36). 48 = 2⁴ × 3, 36 = 2² × 3². LCM = 2⁴ × 3² = 144. The smaller gear (36 teeth) needs 144 ÷ 36 = 4 rotations.

questions.question: A prime number p satisfies the condition that p, p + 2, and p + 6 are all prime. What is the only value of p less than 20 for which this is true?
questions.explanation: Check primes: p = 2: 2, 4, 8 — no. p = 3: 3, 5, 9 — no. p = 5: 5, 7, 11 — yes. p = 7: 7, 9, 13 — no. p = 11: 11, 13, 17 — yes. Both 5 and 11 work; 5 is the smaller.

questions.question: What is the largest positive integer that divides both 144 and 216, but does not divide 90?
questions.explanation: GCD(144, 216): 144 = 2⁴ × 3², 216 = 2³ × 3³. GCD = 2³ × 3² = 72. Check: 72 divides 144 and 216. Does 72 divide 90? 90 = 2 × 3² × 5, so 2³ does not divide 90. Answer: 72.

questions.question: If n is a positive integer such that 2n + 1 is prime and n is divisible by 3, what is the smallest possible value of n?
questions.explanation: n must be divisible by 3: n = 3, 6, 9, 12, ... Check: n = 3 → 2(3) + 1 = 7 (prime) ✓. So n = 3 works.

questions.question: A rectangular garden has dimensions that are factors of 180. If the length is 12 meters, what are all possible integer widths? List them in ascending order separated by commas.
questions.explanation: Both length and width must be factors of 180. Since length = 12, width = 180 ÷ 12 × k where 12k is a factor of 180. Equivalently, width must divide 180 ÷ 12 = 15. Factors of 15: 1, 3, 5, 15.
